name: Langreo by Garganta and Nava by Faya los Llobos – circular from Pola de Siero
name_original: Langreo por Garganta y Nava por Faya los Llobos – circular desde Pola de Siero
region_intro: Road cycling around Siero, located in Asturias, Spain, offers diverse landscapes for cyclists. The region features lush green countryside, rolling hills, and river valleys, providing varied terrain for road cycling. Its central position in Asturias allows access to both gentler routes along rivers like the Nora and more challenging climbs in the broader Asturian mountain ranges. The area is characterized by its verdant environment and agricultural lands. Best road cycling routes around Siero The most popular road cycling route is Langreo by Garganta and Nava by Faya los Llobos – circular from Pola de Siero, a 40.7 miles (65.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 12 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views of the Asturian landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Trubia, Dosango, and Alto del Escamplero Loop from Lugones, a difficult 43.1 miles (69.4 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain, traversing varied terrain with scenic vistas. Local road cyclists also love the Camin de Careses Loop, a 13.2 miles (21.2 km) trail leading through green countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 4 minutes. Road cycling around Siero is defined by lush green landscapes, rolling hills, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to challenging climbs. The routes in Siero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 4000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Siero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Siero?
A: Siero offers a wide variety of road cycling routes, with over 70 documented trails. These routes cater to all skill levels, including 12 easy, 35 moderate, and 26 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable ride for every cyclist.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Siero?
A: Yes, Siero has several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. The region features gentler paths, particularly along river valleys like the Río Nora. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1069395121/camin-de-careses-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camin de Careses Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 21.2 km trail that winds through green countryside, offering a pleasant and accessible ride.

Q: What kind of challenging road cycling routes can I find in Siero?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, Siero's central location in Asturias provides access to demanding terrain. You can find routes with significant elevation gains, traversing varied landscapes. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077544067/langreo-by-garganta-and-nava-by-faya-los-llobos-circular-from-pola-de-siero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Langreo by Garganta and Nava by Faya los Llobos – circular from Pola de Siero&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 65.5 km route with extensive views, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077491694/trubia-dosango-and-alto-del-escamplero-loop-from-lugones" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trubia, Dosango, and Alto del Escamplero Loop from Lugones&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 69.4 km path with over 1200m of ascent.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Siero?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Siero are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1069395121/camin-de-careses-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camin de Careses Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077544067/langreo-by-garganta-and-nava-by-faya-los-llobos-circular-from-pola-de-siero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Langreo by Garganta and Nava by Faya los Llobos – circular from Pola de Siero&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077491694/trubia-dosango-and-alto-del-escamplero-loop-from-lugones" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trubia, Dosango, and Alto del Escamplero Loop from Lugones&lt;/a&gt;, all offering a convenient circular experience.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect while road cycling in Siero?
A: Road cycling in Siero offers lush green landscapes, rolling hills, and picturesque river valleys. The region is known as the 'green jewel of northern Spain,' with routes often passing through verdant areas, traditional Asturian villages, and agricultural lands. You'll experience a blend of natural beauty and cultural sights.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along road cycling routes in Siero?
A: While cycling around Siero, you can encounter various cultural and natural attractions. Highlights include the Church of San Pedro, Valdepino Palace, and Mendoza Palace in Pola de Siero. Further afield, you can reach significant mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5619300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Santo Emiliano&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4429467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Madera&lt;/a&gt;, and even peaks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/210761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacred Heart of Jesus Monument (Monte Naranco)&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning views.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Siero?
A: The road cycling routes in Siero are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from gentle riverside paths to challenging climbs, and the stunning green landscapes that characterize the Asturian region. The variety ensures there's always something new to explore.

Q: Are there any routes that feature significant climbs or mountain passes near Siero?
A: Absolutely. Siero's proximity to the broader Asturian mountain ranges means you can tackle significant climbs. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31915264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Campa – Alto de la Madera loop from Parque Principado&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 103.2 km route, include substantial elevation gains and take you over notable mountain passes. The region is renowned for climbs featured in the Vuelta a España.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Siero?
A: The lush green landscapes of Asturias, including Siero, are enjoyable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. While winter can be cooler, many routes remain accessible, especially those at lower elevations, allowing for year-round cycling opportunities.

Q: Can I find routes that offer viewpoints or scenic overlooks in Siero?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Siero provide excellent viewpoints and scenic overlooks. The region's rolling hills and diverse terrain naturally lead to elevated sections that offer panoramic views of the Asturian landscape. Routes that traverse mountain passes or higher ground, such as those leading towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Praeres – Sierra de Peñamayor&lt;/a&gt;, are particularly rewarding for their vistas.

Q: Are there moderate road cycling routes available for intermediate cyclists?
A: Yes, Siero offers a good selection of moderate road cycling routes, with 35 options available. These routes provide a balanced challenge, suitable for intermediate cyclists looking for more than an easy ride but less demanding than the most difficult climbs. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Camocha – Alto de la Madera loop from Meres&lt;/a&gt;, a 61 km route with a moderate difficulty level.
tips: Beautiful views of the Nalón river as we pass through the city of Langreo at the height of the Miramar bridge.

The Alto de la Faya de los Lobos is a well-known ascent that has been a passing area for the Vuelta a España and the Vuelta a Asturias. A fairly wide road (no shoulder) and in good condition that awaits us with an average slope of 5.37% and a maximum of 8-9% over the 6.7 km of the climb.

Rolling through beautiful Asturia we find ourselves in La Pola Siero, where we can visit the church of San Pedro, the Valdepino palace, the Mendoza palace and the covered square. If we feel like taking a break, we can stop for a glass of cider, since La Pola Siero has a long tradition in cider production.
